---
title: "ForgeLab Carpentries"
subtitle: "Hands-on workshops for robotics, autonomy, and applied AI"
---

Welcome! ForgeLab Carpentries teaches the foundational and advanced skills behind modern
robotics and AI systems — using the [Carpentries](https://carpentries.org/) model of
**participatory live coding**: you don't watch, you build along with the instructor.

## How these lessons work

Each **episode** follows the same rhythm:

1. **Objectives** — what you'll be able to do by the end.
2. **Live coding** — the instructor types; you type along.
3. **Challenges** — short exercises, with solutions you can reveal.
4. **Key points** — a recap you can take away.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Before you start
Work through [Setup](setup.qmd) to install everything *before* your workshop.
Keep [Reference](reference.qmd) open in a tab — it's your cheat sheet.
:::

## Learning tracks

| Track | Episodes start at |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Foundations** — Unix, Bash, Git, CI/CD | [Episode 1](episodes/01-intro-unix-shell.qmd) |
| **Robotics & Autonomy** — ROS 2, navigation | [Episode 3](episodes/03-ros2-navigation.qmd) |
| **AI & World Models** — VLA, VLN, LMs | *(in development — see [curriculum](https://github.com/FrankAsanteVanLaarhoven/ForgeLabCarpentries/tree/main/curriculum))* |

The full curriculum, including Cloud/Deployment, Blockchain, and Capstones, lives in the
[`curriculum/`](https://github.com/FrankAsanteVanLaarhoven/ForgeLabCarpentries/tree/main/curriculum) directory.

## Who this is for

Researchers, engineers, students, and domain experts who want practical, reproducible
robotics/AI skills — no prior systems-programming background assumed for the Foundations track.

## Code of Conduct

All ForgeLab spaces follow our
[Code of Conduct](https://github.com/FrankAsanteVanLaarhoven/ForgeLabCarpentries/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).
Be kind, be patient, and remember: every question is a good question.
